factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Residential Heating & Cooling and Refrigeration. Both segments have shown significant improvements in profit margins, driven by higher sales volumes, lower commodity costs, and factory productivity.

evidence.text: The Residential Heating & Cooling segment's profit margin increased 16% due to higher sales volumes, lower commodity costs, and factory productivity.

evidence.text: The Refrigeration segment's profit margin increased 60.2% due to higher sales volumes and lower commodity costs.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is strong, with acceleration in the second quarter and stable to slight increases across segments year-to-date.

evidence.text: Net sales increased $26 million, or 3%, to $1,019 million in the second quarter of 2016 compared to $993 million in the second quarter of 2015. The Residential Heating & Cooling and Refrigeration segments delivered higher volume by capturing additional replacement and new construction business.

factors.summary: Management is demonstrating confidence in both financial performance and strategic initiatives. They are optimistic about improving margins and cost management, as well as their ability to utilize foreign tax credits and other benefits to sustainably reduce the effective tax rate. Additionally, management is confident in ongoing investments in capital expenditures and share repurchases, supported by higher borrowings to fund these activities.

evidence.text: We expect our annual effective tax rate to be 31% in 2016 and subsequent years to be approximately 32% due to sustainable benefits from restructuring of our international subsidiaries that will enable us to utilize foreign tax credits and other benefits that drives our effective tax rate down by approximately 250 basis points.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of 2016 Compared to Second Quarter of 2015 - Consolidated Results

evidence.text: Net cash provided by financing activities decreased to $97 million... Higher borrowings to support stock repurchases and dividend payments... Completion of $200 million ASR and plans for another $100 million stock repurchase program.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources
